REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF WENDY HEMMING, WENDY HEMMING,S MOTION TO AMEND RESPONSE TO ADMISSION NO 11 IS DENIED. THE INITIAL RESPONSE WAS LITERALLY TRUE AS TO THE POINT IN TIME WHEN HER ADMISSION WAS MADE. HOWEVER, THE ADMISSION DOES NOT PRECLUDE HER FROM INTRODUCING EVIDENCE THAT AT A DIFFERENT POINT IN TIME WINDOWS DID EXIST ON THE NORTH WALL. AN RFA SHOULD NOT BE USED AS A DEVICE TO HIDE OR CONFUSE ISSUES. SEE BURCH V. GOMBOS (2000) 82 CAL.APP.4TH 355, 359-361. SEE ALSO FREDERICH V. KONTOS INDUSTRIES, INC. (1987) 189 CAL.APP. 274. DEFENDANT SUBMITS A PROPOSED ORDER FOR THE COURT'S REVIEW. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (501/CFH)

PLAINTIFF WENDY HEMMING, WENDY HEMMING,s Motion To Amend Response To Admission No 11 is DENIED. The initial response was literally true as to the point in time when her admission was made. However, the admission does not preclude her from introducing evidence that at a different point in time windows did exist on the north wall. An RFA should not be used as a device to hide or confuse issues. See Burch v. Gombos (2000) 82 Cal.App.4th 355, 359-361. See also Frederich v. Kontos Industries, Inc. (1987) 189 Cal.App. 274.

Defendant submits a proposed order for the Court's review.
